note: jquery_ui_button is a passive asset/library provider (no config UI, no permissions, no services, no entities, no plugin types, no .module or .libraries.yml of its own), so it has no introspection or execution surface. There is no setup-config to save and read back (nothing is configurable) and nothing for the agent to build and verify against live state. Per the eval spec, medium (introspection) and hard (execution) tiers are therefore OMITTED as they cannot be authored without fabrication. The single registered library and its assets/dependencies (jquery_ui_button/button -> jQuery UI 1.13.2 button-min.js + base/button.css) are a STATIC fact, so they are covered as easy recipe cases. Facts are grounded in data.json, usage.md, agent/theming/jquery_ui_button.md, and live library.discovery introspection of jquery_ui_button/button.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, why would I install the contrib `jquery_ui_button` module? What does it actually provide?
evals.expected_output: It re-provides the single jQuery UI Button widget asset library as a contrib module after jQuery UI was deprecated and removed from Drupal core, so existing themes/modules that used $.fn.button()/buttonset() keep working. It has no config, permissions or services.

evals.prompt: What is the exact library id provided for the jQuery UI Button widget, and is there a separate buttonset library?
evals.expected_output: The library id is jquery_ui_button/button. There is no separate buttonset library — the button widget covers the legacy buttonset() behavior.

evals.prompt: In a Drupal 11 render array I need the jQuery UI Button widget. Give the exact `#attached` line to attach it.
In my own module's *.libraries.yml, how do I make my JS library depend on the jQuery UI Button widget? Show the YAML.
Does the jquery_ui_button module have its own libraries file, or does it depend on another module to register the Button library? Which modules does it depend on?
evals.expected_output: It has no *.libraries.yml of its own. It depends on the base jquery_ui module (>=8.x-1.7 / composer ^1.7), whose hook_library_info_alter() reads jquery_ui.libraries.data.json and registers the jquery_ui_button/button library and vendored assets. It also depends on jquery_ui_controlgroup and jquery_ui_checkboxradio (>=2.1).

evals.prompt: Where is the configuration/settings page for the jquery_ui_button module, and what permissions does it add?
evals.expected_output: There is none: jquery_ui_button has no configuration UI, no permissions and no services. You simply install it and attach the library you need.

evals.prompt: If I attach jquery_ui_button/button, do I also need to attach jquery_ui/widget, jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup or jquery_ui_checkboxradio/checkboxradio myself?
evals.expected_output: No. The button library declares its dependencies (core/jquery, jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup, jquery_ui_checkboxradio/checkboxradio, jquery_ui/internal.keycode, jquery_ui/widget, jquery_ui/internal.widget-css), so they are pulled in automatically.
